sources.name: competitive_programming_python
sources.license_notes: Mixed upstream licenses documented in the dataset card.
sources.rationale: Strong Python-first competitive programming and code reasoning supervision; directly targets model-forge artifact/code-generation capability.

sources.name: qwen_coder_distill
sources.rationale: Distilled from a strong code-specialist teacher; balances the programming blend with shorter cleaned code tasks.

sources.name: reasoning_700x
sources.license_notes: Apache-2.0 dataset card plus Qwen/DashScope upstream terms.
sources.rationale: Small but high-signal reasoning set used in Jackrong notebooks; included as a continuity baseline rather than the dominant source.

sources.name: logicmind_chat_reasoning
sources.license_notes: See dataset card before public release.
sources.rationale: Multi-turn reasoning/chat coverage to avoid a pure math-code model and to preserve normal assistant behavior.

sources.name: natural_reasoning_gpt_oss_120b
sources.license_notes: See dataset card before public release.
sources.rationale: Natural-language reasoning traces diversify away from programming-heavy examples and should help challenge-capability preservation.

sources.name: stem_reasoning_gpt_oss_120b
sources.rationale: STEM reasoning with explicit final answers; useful for math/science tasks without over-weighting very long math-only traces.

sources.name: sharegpt_reasoning_chat
sources.license_notes: See dataset card before public release.
sources.rationale: General chat/instruction style to reduce regression risk from a narrow reasoning/code blend.

hardware.label: DGX Spark / GB10 unified memory
notes: Use a Spark/GB10-native vLLM build; stock wheels are usually not compiled for SM 12.1.
Conservative DGX Spark cap; raise GPU_MEMORY_UTILIZATION only after smoke serving passes.
Use FP8 KV cache, chunked prefill, prefix caching, and low max-num-seqs to preserve unified-memory headroom.
For NVFP4 MoE, keep routers and multimodal projection/vision modules in BF16 unless model-specific evals prove otherwise.
For CPU/input-pipeline bottlenecks, DGX Spark can benefit from high parallelism such as c=192, but gate it behind an explicit override.

strategy_delta.Keep Jackrong's useful pattern: LoRA/QLoRA SFT over mixed reasoning, coding, math, and chat data.

strategy_delta: Improve over notebook-style mixing with explicit data quality gates, deduplication, holdouts, eval-linked promotion gates, and model-forge artifact preservation.
Avoid training directly on model-forge eval prompts; train adjacent skills and compare on held-out internal, artifact, and external evals.
